title: Dockerfile Frontend, Build Context, Instructions, and Process Contracts · Image and Runtime Lens: Dockerfile Cmd Entrypoint Contract
summary: A small accessible web application needs a readable Dockerfile whose build inputs, defaults, process model, and runtime files are explicit. Planned artifact: Add and verify the dockerfile cmd entrypoint contract increment in reviewed application Dockerfile and build-context contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compose ENTRYPOINT and CMD into an override-friendly PID 1 contract and test arguments, signals, and exit status.
Distinguish build-time RUN from runtime process configuration and choose shell or exec form with deliberate expansion and signal behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.

version-baseline:

  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment

steps.title: Predict Dockerfile Cmd Entrypoint Contract · Theory Dockerfile Cmd Entrypoint Contract
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory build sends a 780 MiB context containing .git and local.env, uses a floating base, copies the repository, runs as root, and wraps the process in /bin/sh. Redesign context and runtime contracts.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Dockerfile Cmd Entrypoint Contract contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example build sends a 780 MiB context containing .git and local.env, uses a floating base, copies the repository, runs as root, and wraps the process in /bin/sh. Redesign context and runtime contracts. Competency probe: Compose ENTRYPOINT and CMD into an override-friendly PID 1 contract and test arguments, signals, and exit status. A defensible solution applies Dockerfile Cmd Entrypoint Contract, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to reviewed application Dockerfile and build-context contract.

content.code:
# Worked focus: dockerfile-cmd-entrypoint-contract — Compose ENTRYPOINT and CMD into an override-friendly PID 1 contract and test arguments, signals, and exit status.
  # Evidence: docker-worked-dockerfile-build-context-instructions-3
  - competency: docker-worked-dockerfile-build-context-instructions-3
    artifact: Dockerfile
    decision: worked dockerfile build context instructions 3
    scenario-variant: 2
    environment: IPv6-enabled Compose project on a shared CI runner
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      FROM docker.io/library/alpine:3.22@sha256:verified AS runtime
      WORKDIR /app
      COPY --link public/ ./public/
      USER 10001:10001
      ENTRYPOINT ["/app/status"]
    verify: docker buildx build --check --progress=plain .
    failure: Reject a floating base, secret-bearing context, shell-form signal wrapper, root runtime, or copied repository wildcard.
    repair: Pin the reviewed base digest, shrink .dockerignore context, use explicit COPY, set numeric non-root identity, and test argv behavior.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Multiple CMD instructions combine into a sequence of default commands. This fails the Dockerfile Cmd Entrypoint Contract contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
